channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Iran's Big Claim On Deal To Reopen Hormuz, US Calls It "Fabrication"

media:content.media:description: The draft also mentioned that the strait will not be reopened "unconditionally"

item.description: The Iranian state television cited what it said was the draft of a potential "memorandum of understanding". However, it clarified that the text had not been "finalised" as of yet.
item.content:encoded: The Iranian state television cited what it said was the draft of a potential "memorandum of understanding". However, it clarified that the text had not been "finalised" as of yet.

item.title: Xi Vowed To Curb Smoking, But Cigarette Sales Tell A Different China Story

media:content.media:description: China sits at the centre of the tobacco industry, with 2.44 trillion cigarettes sold annually

item.description: China's cigarette packaging also looks strikingly different from that in many countries. Instead of graphic health warnings, packs typically feature a single-line caution alongside national imagery such as pandas or the Gate of Heavenly Peace.
item.content:encoded: China's cigarette packaging also looks strikingly different from that in many countries. Instead of graphic health warnings, packs typically feature a single-line caution alongside national imagery such as pandas or the Gate of Heavenly Peace.

item.title: Russian Lawmakers Want Banks, Their Staff To Help Fight Ukrainian Drones

media:content.media:description: Russia is finding it hard to protect its large land mass from a growing number of attacks

item.description: Russian bank staff and branches could be joining the fight against Ukrainian drone attacks under an ambitious plan approved by the country's lower house of parliament.
item.content:encoded: Russian bank staff and branches could be joining the fight against Ukrainian drone attacks under an ambitious plan approved by the country's lower house of parliament.

item.title: Iran Says "Low" Possibility Of Return To War With US

media:content.media:description: The statement came a day after Iran accused the US of breaching the ceasefire

item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Wednesday that a return to war with the United States was unlikely, but warned the Islamic republic stood ready to respond to any attack.
item.content:encoded: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Wednesday that a return to war with the United States was unlikely, but warned the Islamic republic stood ready to respond to any attack.

item.title: West Bank On Edge As Israeli Army Steps Up Vigil In Samaria Amid Regional Tensions

media:content.media:description: The area around Peduel remains one of the closely monitored sectors in the region

item.description: NDTV travelled to Peduel in Samaria, where Israeli authorities say security forces are maintaining round the clock vigilance amid fears of infiltration attempts and attacks targeting Jewish communities in and around the West Bank.
item.content:encoded: NDTV travelled to Peduel in Samaria, where Israeli authorities say security forces are maintaining round the clock vigilance amid fears of infiltration attempts and attacks targeting Jewish communities in and around the West Bank.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | How Pak And Bangladeshi Vote Blocs Are Becoming UK's New 'Kingmakers'

media:content.media:description: In Tower Hamlets, which is 40% Bangladeshi-origin Muslim, Lutfur Rahman's Aspire party won 33 council seats out of 45, all held by Bangladeshi Muslims.

item.description: In a February by-election in Gorton and Denton, a Manchester suburb, Pakistani voters played a major role in helping the Green Party come out on top, pushing Labour into third place behind Reform.
item.content:encoded: In a February by-election in Gorton and Denton, a Manchester suburb, Pakistani voters played a major role in helping the Green Party come out on top, pushing Labour into third place behind Reform.

item.title: UK Suspends Indian-Origin Doctor. He Slept With Patient, Gave Her Opioids

media:content.media:description: Patel had been a permanent consultant at the hospital since 2018.

item.description: The accused doctor, Chirag Patel, a consultant neurosurgeon at Cardiff's University Hospital of Wales, claimed to have prescribed opioid painkillers to a woman named only as 'Patient A' because she "blackmailed" him with threats of exposing their rel
item.content:encoded: The accused doctor, Chirag Patel, a consultant neurosurgeon at Cardiff's University Hospital of Wales, claimed to have prescribed opioid painkillers to a woman named only as 'Patient A' because she "blackmailed" him with threats of exposing their rel

item.title: US-Iran War LIVE: Iran Says War With US Unlikely, Warns It Is Ready To Respond
item.description: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.
item.content:encoded: Iran War: The US military on Monday carried out strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats laying mines.

item.title: Woman Missing Since 1978 May Have Been Kidnapped, Turned Spy In North Korea: Report

media:content.media:description: Leidy's name had surfaced years ago in connection with North Korea's kidnapping network

item.description: Aleida Maria Anderske Kaspersma, known as Leidy, vanished in July 1978 while living near the Irish town of Kenmare with her British boyfriend Nick.
item.content:encoded: Aleida Maria Anderske Kaspersma, known as Leidy, vanished in July 1978 while living near the Irish town of Kenmare with her British boyfriend Nick.

item.title: Nvidia To Invest Rs 12,000 Crore In 'Epicentre Of AI Revolution' Taiwan

media:content.media:description: Taiwan is a powerhouse in the manufacturing of semiconductors

item.description: Jensen Huang, the chief executive of the world's most valuable company, said "Taiwan is booming" and Nvidia's investment, up from $100 billion, will "fuel an incredible ecosystem here".
item.content:encoded: Jensen Huang, the chief executive of the world's most valuable company, said "Taiwan is booming" and Nvidia's investment, up from $100 billion, will "fuel an incredible ecosystem here".

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Stray Into Baltic Airspace, Raising Tensions With Russia

media:content.media:description: Damage to an oil tank after drones crashed at a storage facility in Rezekne, Latvia

item.description: The airspace incursions have occurred as Ukraine, seeking to land heavier blows on Russia four years after Moscow's full-scale invasion, uses exploding drones to hit Russian Baltic ports that handle nearly 40% of national oil and gas exports.
item.content:encoded: The airspace incursions have occurred as Ukraine, seeking to land heavier blows on Russia four years after Moscow's full-scale invasion, uses exploding drones to hit Russian Baltic ports that handle nearly 40% of national oil and gas exports.

item.title: Tibet-In-Exile Leader Penpa Tsering Sworn In For Second Term

media:content.media:description: The government's "sikyong", or leader, Penpa Tsering, was elected for a second term

item.description: Penpa Tsering, was elected for a second term, after taking 61% in the preliminary round -- a high enough threshold to win outright.
item.content:encoded: Penpa Tsering, was elected for a second term, after taking 61% in the preliminary round -- a high enough threshold to win outright.

item.title: Matthew Perry's Assistant Last To Be Sentenced In Ketamine Drug Death Case

media:content.media:description: The actor had been taking the surgical anesthetic ketamine legally for depression

item.description: Kenneth Iwamasa, 60, is set to be sentenced Wednesday in the Los Angeles federal courtroom of Judge Sherilyn Peace Garnett, who has sentenced four of his co-defendants in the past year.
item.content:encoded: Kenneth Iwamasa, 60, is set to be sentenced Wednesday in the Los Angeles federal courtroom of Judge Sherilyn Peace Garnett, who has sentenced four of his co-defendants in the past year.

item.title: 78,000 Samsung Workers Set For Rs 3 Crore Bonuses After Big AI Profits

media:content.media:description: Members of Samsung Electronics labour staged a rally demanding removal of cap on performance bonuses

item.description: Around 78,000 employees from the company's 125,000-strong domestic workforce are eligible to receive a bonus of roughly $370,000 this year
item.content:encoded: Around 78,000 employees from the company's 125,000-strong domestic workforce are eligible to receive a bonus of roughly $370,000 this year

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | Pakistan's Dangerous Dance With America Is Finally Starting To Haunt It
item.description: A fanatical opposition to Israel has been assiduously cultivated by successive regimes in Pakistan, so much so that anti-Semitism has been normalised. A U-turn could cause massive unrest.
item.content:encoded: A fanatical opposition to Israel has been assiduously cultivated by successive regimes in Pakistan, so much so that anti-Semitism has been normalised. A U-turn could cause massive unrest.

item.title: Ex-JPMorgan Banker's Lawyer Withdraws Hours Before Sex Abuse Case Hearing

media:content.media:description: Chirayu Rana had accused Lorna Hajdini of sexually abusing, drugging and repeatedly harassing him.

item.description: Court documents revealed that Rana does not currently have another lawyer to represent him.
item.content:encoded: Court documents revealed that Rana does not currently have another lawyer to represent him.

item.title: Exclusive: World's Newest Country Is Without Medicine. And It's Getting Worse

media:content.media:description: MSF has publicly accused South Sudanese authorities of blocking humanitarian access.

item.description: For the people of South Sudan, the destruction of an MSF-run hospital meant the disappearance of the only secondary healthcare facility serving nearly 250,000 people across the region.
item.content:encoded: For the people of South Sudan, the destruction of an MSF-run hospital meant the disappearance of the only secondary healthcare facility serving nearly 250,000 people across the region.

item.title: The China Factor In The New India-US Critical Minerals Deal

media:content.media:description: US Secretary of State Rubio signed the deal in India

item.description: The United States and other nations depend heavily on China for these minerals. But in recent years, especially under President Donald Trump's administration, Washington has pushed to diversify its sourcing to reduce reliance on Beijing.
item.content:encoded: The United States and other nations depend heavily on China for these minerals. But in recent years, especially under President Donald Trump's administration, Washington has pushed to diversify its sourcing to reduce reliance on Beijing.

item.title: NASA Rolls Out Moon Base Plan, Signs Deals For Landers, Rovers, Drones

media:content.media:description: Carlos Garcia-Galan (L), NASA Moon Base programme executive outlines plans for NASA's "Moon Base"

item.description: The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four US companies.
item.content:encoded: The space agency outlined the first phase of its moon base plans on Tuesday, awarding hundreds of millions of dollars in contracts to four US companies.

item.title: Trump Team Proposes Non-Disclosure Agreements For Staff To Stop Media Leaks

media:content.media:description: The proposed notice seeks comment on several questions

item.description: A proposed notice is expected to be officially published seeking comment on a draft NDA to be used by federal agencies for "both new and existing employees."
item.content:encoded: A proposed notice is expected to be officially published seeking comment on a draft NDA to be used by federal agencies for "both new and existing employees."
